description:
This diagram illustrates the ascending and descending phases of a satellite's orbit around Earth, showing the direction of both the satellite's path and its ground track for data acquisition.
The left panel shows the "Ascending orbit". The satellite moves from the South pole (S) towards the North pole (N), indicated by a red curved arrow. The yellow arrow depicts the satellite's ground track for data acquisition, covering areas from the South Atlantic/West Africa, across the Atlantic Ocean, and towards Europe.
The right panel shows the "Descending orbit". The satellite moves from the North pole (N) towards the South pole (S), indicated by a red curved arrow. The yellow arrow depicts the satellite's ground track for data acquisition, covering areas from northern Europe/Asia, across the Mediterranean Sea, towards North Africa.
